entries.title: What is CSV / Formula Injection
entries.body:
CSV Injection (also known as Formula Injection) occurs when unvalidated user input ends up in an exported CSV file. Many applications let you download data (invoices, settings, templates, reports) as CSV, and a user opens it in Excel / LibreOffice / OpenOffice. If cell content is not sanitized, a cell that begins with a special character is treated as a formula and executed on the victim's machine - up to RCE via DDE.

The essence of the attack: you do not break the server - you inject a payload into data that another user (often an admin exporting a report) will later open in their spreadsheet editor.

entries.title: Where to look for formula injection
entries.body:
• Any export-to-CSV / XLSX feature: user lists, orders, logs, feedback, contacts, reports.
• Fields that you control and that will later end up in an exported table: first name, last name, address, comment, ticket subject, company name, User-Agent, referrer.
• Feedback forms and tickets that an admin exports and opens in Excel.
• Import-export round trips: you entered a value → it was exported to CSV → someone opened it.
• Blind testing: the value will be opened not by you, so use out-of-band payloads (see exfiltration via Google Sheets).

rows: The main trigger in all editors
Used in DDE and function names
Carriage return as a prefix to bypass filters

entries.title: A nuance with the hyphen in the source
entries.body: In the original PayloadsAllTheThings the trigger list includes the character – (en-dash, U+2013) rather than the regular minus -. This is a typo in the source: the actual formula trigger is the minus - (U+002D), not the typographic dash. When testing, use exactly =, +, -, @. The full list of leading characters that OWASP escapes: = + - @ TAB(0x09) CR(0x0D).

entries.title: How a DDE payload is structured
entries.body:
Breakdown of a payload of the form =cmd|' /C calc'!A0:
• cmd - the name of the DDE server application that the client (Excel) contacts.
• /C calc - the command / file name being passed (here calc.exe).
• !A0 - the item name, the unit of data the server returns to the client on request.

DDE (Dynamic Data Exchange) is a mechanism for exchanging data between Windows applications; Excel executes the external command when the file is opened (usually after a warning about enabling content).

entries.title: Null characters to bypass dictionary filters
entries.body:
Null characters (not spaces) are ignored during execution, so they break signature/dictionary filters without preventing the formula from triggering:

=    C    m D                    |        '/        c       c  al  c      .  e                  x       e  '   !   A

entries.title: Google Sheets functions for requesting remote URLs
entries.body:
Google Sheets supports formulas that pull external resources - they can be used for blind injection and data exfiltration:

IMPORTXML(url, xpath_query, locale)
IMPORTRANGE(spreadsheet_url, range_string)
IMPORTHTML(url, query, index)
IMPORTFEED(url, [query], [headers], [num_items])
IMPORTDATA(url)

entries.title: Blind formula injection / exfiltration via IMPORTXML
Warning shown to the user
entries.body: Google Sheets will show the user a warning that the formula is trying to access an external resource and will request authorization. This reduces the chance of it triggering blindly, but given the user's trust/inattention, exfiltration is still possible.
